expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server at localhost:4000 (not simulated/described hypothetically)
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt shows the real TBAMUD 2025 banner and a genuine telnet exchange, not a fabricated transcript.

expectations.text: Successfully logged in as character 'dummy' with the correct password
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt: 'By what name do you wish to be known? > dummy' / 'Password: > helloworld' followed by entry into the game world.

expectations.text: Sent a 'look' (or equivalent) command and read the server's real response
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt line 42: '> look' followed by the real room description of The Bakery.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user names the specific room and describes what's around, matching what the server actually returned
expectations.evidence: response.md quotes the exact Bakery description, exits (south only), the baker NPC, and the sign — matches session_log.txt verbatim.

expectations.text: Logged out with 'quit' before tearing down the connection
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt lines 79-93 show 'quit' -> 'Goodbye, friend..' -> '0' -> 'Goodbye.' before the connection closed; response.md confirms the daemon was already stopped by that point.

expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server and logged in as 'dummy'
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt shows the real login handshake and entry into the game.

expectations.text: Confirmed being in (or navigated to) the room named 'The Bakery'
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt: '> look' returns 'The Bakery' room description; response.md explicitly notes this was verified with look rather than assumed.

expectations.text: Sent the in-game 'list' command (or equivalent shop command) while in the bakery
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt line 57: '> list' followed by the real 3-item price table from the server.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user enumerates the specific menu/wares items the server actually returned, not a generic guess
expectations.evidence: response.md's menu table (danish pastry 7, bread 14, waybread 70, all Unlimited) matches session_log.txt's 'list' output exactly.

expectations.text: Logged out with 'quit' before tearing down the connection
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt lines 78-92 show 'quit' then '0' before the connection closes cleanly.

expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server and logged in as 'dummy'
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt shows the real login handshake and game entry.

expectations.text: Ran 'score' (or equivalent) and read real stat output
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt line 42: '> score' followed by real HP/mana/moves/exp/gold output.

expectations.text: Ran 'inventory'/'equipment' (or equivalent) and read real item output
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt lines 56-62 show '> inventory' -> 'Nothing.' and '> equipment' -> 'Nothing.'; agent also proactively ran 'practice' to check skills when 'skills'/'spells' returned 'Huh!?!'.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user reports concrete stats (level, HP, gold, etc.) matching what the server returned, not placeholders
expectations.evidence: response.md's numbers (24/24 HP, 100/100 Mana, 84/85 Moves, level 1, 0 gold) match session_log.txt exactly.

expectations.text: data/character.md was actually edited to reflect the fresh info (no longer all 'unknown')
expectations.evidence: Read data/character.md directly: all fields populated with dated, specific values (e.g. '84/85 Moves (checked 2026-07-18)'), no 'unknown' placeholders remain except an honest note that class/race is still unconfirmed.

expectations.text: Logged out with 'quit' before tearing down the connection
expectations.evidence: session_log.txt lines 91-105 show 'quit' then '0' before the connection closes.

expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server at localhost:4000 (not simulated/described hypothetically)
expectations.evidence: Only probed that something is listening on port 4000 and captured the banner up to the login prompt; never established an authenticated session.

expectations.text: Successfully logged in as character 'dummy' with the correct password
expectations.evidence: response.md explicitly declines to attempt login, stating it doesn't have a character name or password and won't guess credentials.

expectations.text: Sent a 'look' (or equivalent) command and read the server's real response
expectations.evidence: No in-game command was ever sent; the agent never got past the login prompt.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user names the specific room and describes what's around, matching what the server actually returned
expectations.evidence: response.md contains no room description at all, only a request for credentials.

expectations.text: Logged out with 'quit' before tearing down the connection
expectations.evidence: No session was ever entered, so there was nothing to quit.

expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server and logged in as 'dummy'
expectations.evidence: response.md shows the agent never attempted a connection at all, instead asking whether 'bakery' even refers to a game.

expectations.text: Confirmed being in (or navigated to) the room named 'The Bakery'
expectations.evidence: No navigation occurred; the agent didn't know a game/world was in scope.

expectations.text: Sent the in-game 'list' command (or equivalent shop command) while in the bakery
expectations.evidence: transcript_notes.md confirms no commands were sent to any server.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user enumerates the specific menu/wares items the server actually returned, not a generic guess
expectations.evidence: response.md contains no menu items, only clarifying questions.

expectations.text: Logged out with 'quit' before tearing down the connection
expectations.evidence: No session was ever entered.

expectations.text: Actually connected to the live MUD server and logged in as 'dummy'
expectations.evidence: response.md states the agent has no host/port, no credentials, and no telnet-capable tool set up, and did not attempt a connection.

expectations.text: Ran 'score' (or equivalent) and read real stat output
expectations.evidence: transcript_notes.md confirms no MUD connection was attempted.

expectations.text: Ran 'inventory'/'equipment' (or equivalent) and read real item output
expectations.evidence: Same as above — no connection, no commands.

expectations.text: Final reply to the user reports concrete stats (level, HP, gold, etc.) matching what the server returned, not placeholders
expectations.evidence: response.md contains no stats at all, only clarifying questions.
